questions.question: Explain the role of splitters in Sample Sort and how they are determined.
keyPoints: Splitters define the boundaries between data buckets
Each process selects samples from its locally sorted data
Root process gathers and sorts all samples
Splitters are chosen at regular intervals from sorted samples
Splitters ensure approximately equal-sized data distribution

questions.question: Compare and contrast Sample Sort and Bitonic Sort in terms of their suitability for different parallel computing scenarios.
keyPoints: Sample Sort works with any number of processes, Bitonic requires power-of-two
Sample Sort uses global sampling for load balancing
Bitonic Sort has regular communication patterns suitable for specific network topologies
Sample Sort generally has better scalability for large datasets
Bitonic Sort has predictable performance but may suffer from load imbalance
Communication complexity differences between the two algorithms
